summary: First testing run for Taqada MVP. Backend: 15/17 pytest passed (88%). Two stub-classifier bugs found for exact prompts specified in review. Frontend: dashboard, widgets, control strip, invoice ledger, filters, drawer tabs, and Rung 3 statutory citation gate all work correctly end-to-end via preview URL.

critical.issue: Stub classifier in llm.py::_stub_classify does NOT classify 'This invoice is wrong, we didn\'t order this' as dispute. Returns 'unclear'. Keyword list checks for 'did not order' (no contraction) and 'dispute'/'wrong amount'/'not correct'/'incorrect'; none match the required test phrase. Fix: add 'wrong' and 'didn\'t order' / broaden keywords. Explicitly listed in review spec.

Stub classifier does NOT classify "I'll pay in 6 months" as promise_to_pay. Returns 'unclear' because the promise heuristic looks for 'will pay' (not the contraction 'I\'ll pay') and no other keyword matches. As a result, the MAX_EXTENSION_DAYS=30 counter-offer path is never reached for this exact test phrase. Fix: add "'ll pay", 'pay in', 'in N months', or a regex-based fallback in _stub_classify.

integration_issues.flow: Simulate debtor reply — long promise / dispute
integration_issues.issue: Because of stub classifier bugs above, the exact prompts in the review ("I'll pay in 6 months", "This invoice is wrong, we didn't order this") route to 'unclear'. After 2 consecutive unclears the flow escalates the invoice — observed INV-2351 in Human Escalation Queue on dashboard for this reason. Fix upstream in _stub_classify.

issues: Radix DialogContent missing DialogTitle / aria-describedby — accessibility warnings in console. Wrap with VisuallyHidden or add DialogTitle.

action_items: Fix llm.py::_stub_classify: broaden 'dispute' keywords (add 'wrong', "didn't order") and promise keywords (add "'ll pay", 'pay in', regex for 'in N months/weeks/days').
Add DialogTitle (or VisuallyHidden wrapper) to all Radix DialogContent instances to silence a11y warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: aging.py compound-interest block computes `growth = (1+r)**months` twice inside try/except — first call runs unconditionally and would raise on Decimal**Decimal-with-fraction pre-Python-3.11; remove the duplicate line 89 for clarity.
agent.py run_agent_tick chases every overdue invoice on each tick without any per-invoice cooldown — repeated calls will spam identical messages. Consider throttling by last chase_event timestamp.
server.py mixes routing and business logic (~400 lines). If server grows, split routers by resource (invoices/, dashboard/, agent/).
Two consecutive 'unclear' replies auto-escalate — with the stub classifier bug this fires very easily. Consider requiring >=2 messages AND some time gap.
llm.py stub uses datetime.utcnow() (deprecated in 3.12) — replace with datetime.now(timezone.utc).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py runs via `python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -o addopts='' --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` (must clear -n xdist from pytest.ini for JUnit). The two failing tests target the exact stub-classifier bugs; keep them as regression checks. Frontend rung 3 gated message renders 'Gate: {reason}' as a separate label above preview-message-body, not inside it — do not assert gate text inside preview-message-body selector.
rca of the issue: Root cause: llm._stub_classify uses a small hard-coded keyword list that does not cover common English contractions ('I\'ll', 'didn\'t'). Since FIREWORKS_API_KEY is empty, stub is the only path. Reproduction: POST /api/agent/simulate-reply {invoice_id, text: "I'll pay in 6 months"} → intent='unclear' (expected promise_to_pay with 30-day counter-offer). Mitigation: extend keyword lists and add a light regex for time expressions ('in 6 months', '6 weeks', 'end of the month').
